name: Drunken Turkey
recipeIngredient: 1 can (92.5 oz) chipotles in adobo sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325°F.
2. Rinse the turkey and pat dry inside and out; set aside.
3. In a large bowl, combine mixed dried fruit, raisins, and apples.
4. Add orange juice and chipotle peppers (with their juices) to a blender or food processor; process for 1 minute.
5. Stir in tequila and Grand Marnier; let mixture rest with the fruit for 15 minutes.
6. Drain the fruit, reserving the liquid. Chop half of the butter into ½-inch pieces and mix with the fruit.
7. Stuff the turkey cavity with most of the fruit mixture.
8. Place remaining fruit on top of the bird and pour reserved juice-liquor mixture over it.
9. Combine any remaining tequila and Grand Marnier.
10. Inject the turkey all over with a basting syringe fitted with a metal nozzle, being careful to avoid steam exposure.
11. Seal the roasting bag; make a small slit on top for steam release.
12. Roast in a preheated oven at 325°F for 2½ hours, injecting more liquor every 30 minutes until ready to serve.
13. Carefully open the bag and arrange turkey on a platter; scoop out the fruit stuffing.
14. Place remaining fruit in a serving bowl; strain juices from the bag, remove any fat, and serve in a gravy boat.
15. Serves 8–10
